steps.title: Identify the cause-effect relationship
steps.content: The first sentence establishes that millions of people use the metro annually. The second sentence describes maintenance efforts by city officials. Heavy usage (cause) necessitates maintenance (effect).

steps.title: Match the transition showing consequence
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-and-effect: heavy ridership creates the need for ongoing maintenance. The blank needs a transition word signaling that the maintenance effort is a natural consequence of the demand.
  • Choice A — "Accordingly,": signals that what follows is a logical consequence of what came before, so it fits — officials maintain the system because of the millions of trips it handles. ✓
  • Choice B — "Alternatively,": signals a different option being offered instead of the first, so it fails because maintenance is not an alternative to high ridership. ✘
  • Choice C — "For example,": signals a specific instance illustrating a general claim, so it fails because the maintenance is not an example of high ridership — it is a response to it. ✘
  • Choice D — "However,": signals contrast or surprise, so it fails because maintenance in response to heavy use is expected, not contradictory. ✘
That matches choice A — A is correct.
